Title: Check scheduled jobs list should be displayed on Dashboard page
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Navigate to EkoAI Console
2. Click Scheduled Jobs menu
3. Observe the dashboard page
Expected Result:
1. Dashboard page loads successfully
2. Job list displays with columns: name / status / schedule / last run / next run
3. Each job shows enable/disable toggle

Title: Check job details page should open when clicking on a job row
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Dashboard
2. Click on any scheduled job row in the list
Expected Result:
1. Page navigates to Job Configuration page
2. Full job details are displayed including name / schedule / endpoint

Title: Check job list should be filtered correctly when selecting status from dropdown
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Open Dashboard with multiple jobs (enabled and disabled)
2. Click on the Menu List dropdown
3. Select a filter option
Expected Result:
1. Dropdown shows available filter options
2. Job list updates to show only matching jobs
3. Non-matching jobs are hidden

Title: Check empty state should be shown when no scheduled jobs exist
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Dashboard when no jobs have been created
2. Observe the page content
Expected Result:
1. Empty state illustration or message is displayed
2. Guidance text suggests creating a new job
3. Create button is accessible

Title: Check error state should be displayed when Dashboard data fails to load
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Dashboard while backend is unavailable
2. Observe the page content
Expected Result:
1. Error message is displayed indicating data load failure
2. Retry option or guidance is shown
3. Page does not crash or show blank content

Title: Check Dashboard should handle 20+ jobs with pagination or scrolling
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Create more than 20 scheduled jobs
2. Navigate to Dashboard
3. Scroll or paginate through the list
Expected Result:
1. Menu List component shows up to 20 items per view
2. Pagination or scroll is available for overflow
3. All jobs are accessible

Title: Check collapsed navbar should render correctly on Dashboard page
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Dashboard
2. Observe the left navigation bar
Expected Result:
1. Left navbar renders in Collapse variant per Figma design
2. Menu items are accessible via expand/collapse
3. Dashboard content area adjusts properly

Title: Check create wizard should open when clicking Create button on Dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Dashboard
2. Click the Create button
Expected Result:
1. Step 1 of create wizard is displayed
2. Main container with form fields is visible
3. Job name field is focused or ready for input

Title: Check job should be created successfully when all required fields are filled
Test Data: Valid job name / HTTPS endpoint / cron expression / audience
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Open create wizard
2. Enter job name and description
3. Configure cron trigger expression
4. Enter HTTPS process endpoint and API key
5. Select MORNING_BRIEF action type
6. Add audience user IDs
7. Click Create/Save button
Expected Result:
1. Job is created and persisted as ScheduleJob entity
2. Toast notification with Status=Success appears
3. User is redirected to job detail or dashboard

Title: Check cron expression should be accepted when entering valid schedule format
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Open create wizard
2. Navigate to trigger configuration step
3. Enter cron expression '0 8 * * 1-5'
Expected Result:
1. Cron expression is accepted without error
2. System interprets as 'weekdays at 08:00'
3. Schedule preview displays correctly

Title: Check repeat options should be displayed when clicking repeat dropdown
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Open create wizard
2. Navigate to trigger config step
3. Click the repeat dropdown
Expected Result:
1. Dropdown Menu List opens
2. Repeat options are shown (daily / weekly / custom cron)
3. User can select an option

Title: Check audience should be saved when adding user IDs and directory IDs

Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console with admin access
Steps:
1. Open create wizard
2. Navigate to audience step
3. Add user IDs to audience
4. Add directory IDs to audience
5. Save configuration
Expected Result:
1. Audience configuration saved with type='Eko'
2. userIds array contains added users
3. directoriesIds array contains added directories

Title: Check action type should be saved when selecting MORNING_BRIEF

1. Open create wizard
2. Navigate to action configuration step
3. Select action type = MORNING_BRIEF
4. Configure schedule (immediate or time-triggered)
Expected Result:
1. Action type MORNING_BRIEF is selected
2. Schedule configuration options appear
3. Action step is saved with type and schedule

Title: Check validation error should appear when submitting without required fields

Steps:

2. Leave name field empty
3. Skip endpoint configuration
4. Click Create/Save button
Expected Result:

2. Validation errors shown for missing required fields (name / endpoint / audience)

Title: Check HTTPS validation should reject when entering HTTP endpoint URL

2. Navigate to process endpoint step
3. Enter HTTP (non-HTTPS) endpoint URL

1. Validation error message appears
2. Error states endpoint must use HTTPS

Title: Check validation error should appear when entering invalid cron expression

2. Navigate to trigger config step
3. Enter invalid cron expression

Expected Result:
1. Validation error is displayed
2. Error indicates invalid cron format

Title: Check default timeout should be 100 seconds when user does not modify timeout field

Title: Check end date picker should accept a single date selection

Title: Check runUntilTimes should accept only positive integers

4. Observe validation
Expected Result:

2. Non-numeric values are rejected
3. Positive integer (e.g. 3) is accepted

Title: Check users should be added when entering Eko user IDs in edit mode
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists

3. Click Save

Title: Check directories should be added when entering Eko directory IDs

Steps:

1. Directory IDs are added to audience.directoriesIds array

3. Toast notification confirms save

Title: Check audience should be resolved to individual users at trigger time
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Configure audience with both user IDs and directory IDs
2. Wait for scheduled job to trigger
Expected Result:
1. System calls Eko APIs to resolve directories into individual users
2. Deleted/disabled users are filtered out
3. Final audience list contains only active users

Title: Check Eko API should be called for user resolution when job triggers
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Configure job with userIds
2. Trigger the job
Expected Result:
1. EkoAI calls Eko APIs (Query Users / Get Users by IDs)
2. Uses Network.ekoConfig for authentication
3. User details are fetched and stored in run snapshot

Title: Check Eko API should be called for directory resolution when job triggers with directoryIds
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Configure job with directoryIds
2. Trigger the job
Expected Result:
1. EkoAI calls Eko APIs (Query Directories / Query Users from Directory)
2. Directory groups are resolved into individual user IDs
3. Resolved users are added to the audience snapshot

Title: Check deleted Eko users should be excluded from audience at trigger time
Test Data: Audience includes a user that was deleted on Eko
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Configure audience with a user ID
2. Delete that user on Eko platform
3. Trigger the scheduled job
Expected Result:
1. Deleted user is excluded from ScheduleJobRun.scheduleJobAudience
2. Job processes only active users
3. No error is thrown for the excluded user

Title: Check job should fail gracefully when Eko API is unavailable during audience resolution
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Configure job with audience
2. Make Eko API unavailable
3. Trigger the job
Expected Result:
1. Audience resolution fails with API error
2. Run is marked as failed or retries gracefully
3. Error details are logged for troubleshooting

Title: Check audience snapshot should remain static during an active job run
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Trigger a job run with configured audience
2. After run starts / modify the audience on Eko (add/remove users)
3. Observe the running job
Expected Result:
1. Running job uses the snapshot taken at trigger time
2. New/removed users do not affect the current run
3. Changes only apply to next trigger

Title: Check audience type should always show Eko in Phase 1
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Recipients tab
2. Observe the audience type field
Expected Result:
1. Audience type displays 'Eko'
2. No other audience type options are available
3. Field is read-only or pre-selected

Title: Check bulk operations modal should open when managing audience
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:

2. Click manage audience button
3. Observe the modal
Expected Result:
1. Modal (Desktop variant) opens
2. Bulk add/remove options are available for users and directories
3. Modal allows batch operations

Title: Check history logs should display past runs with status and timestamps
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job with past runs exists

Expected Result:

2. Each run shows status (RUNNING/SUCCESS/FAILED)

Title: Check per-user execution details should be visible when clicking on a run entry

Title: Check failed executions should be re-enqueued when clicking Retry

Expected Result:
1. Retrying state is shown

Title: Check ScheduleJobRun aggregated status should display after run completes
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:

3. Observe the run status
Expected Result:
1. ScheduleJobRun shows aggregated status: SUCCESS or FAILED
2. Status reflects all per-user executions
3. Timestamp shows completion time

Title: Check per-user status transitions should be tracked in history
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Trigger a job run
2. Navigate to History Logs
3. Expand a run to see per-user details
Expected Result:
1. Each user shows status progression
2. Transitions visible: PENDING > PROCESSING > EXECUTING > SUCCESS/FAILED
3. Timestamps for each transition are recorded

Title: Check empty state should be shown when job has no past runs
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to History Logs for a newly created job with no runs
2. Observe the page content
Expected Result:
1. Empty state frame is displayed
2. Appropriate message indicates no runs yet
3. No table rows are shown

Title: Check run detail modal should handle missing data gracefully
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:

2. Click on a run entry with incomplete data
Expected Result:
1. Modal (Type=Basic modal) opens
2. Available data is displayed
3. Missing fields show placeholder or N/A without errors

Title: Check select all should work for entries on current page
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to History Logs with multiple entries
2. Click 'Select all' > 'This page'
Expected Result:
1. All visible entries on current page are selected
2. Selection checkboxes are checked
3. Bulk action buttons become available

Title: Check select all should handle 400 audience entries across pages
Preconditions: User is logged in to EkoAI Console / A scheduled job exists
Steps:
1. View job run with 400 audience members
2. Click 'Select all'
3. Confirm all entries selection
Expected Result:
1. All 400 audience entries are selected across pages
2. UI handles large selection without freezing
3. Bulk actions apply to all selected entries

Title: Check scheduler should pick up jobs when nextRun time is due
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Create an enabled scheduled job with nextRun in the past or now
2. Wait for the scheduler tick to execute
Expected Result:
1. Scheduler identifies the due job
2. ScheduleJobRun is created with config + audience snapshot
3. Job processing begins

Title: Check nextRun should be updated after each successful trigger
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Trigger a job with cron '0 8 * * 1-5'
2. Check the nextRun value after trigger
Expected Result:
1. nextRun is recalculated based on iCalendar definition
2. New nextRun points to next weekday at 08:00
3. lastRun is updated to current trigger time

Title: Check job run snapshot should contain frozen config at trigger time
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Trigger a job run
2. Inspect the created ScheduleJobRun record
Expected Result:
1. ScheduleJobRun.scheduleJobStep contains frozen snapshot
2. Trigger / process / action config are all captured
3. Snapshot does not change even if job config is modified later

Title: Check internal trigger endpoint should process eligible jobs
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. POST to /_internal/scheduled-job-action-orchestrator/trigger
2. Observe response
Expected Result:
1. Endpoint processes eligible jobs within the tick interval (~1 minute)
2. Returns success response
3. Due jobs are picked up and run creation begins

Title: Check scheduler should skip jobs when isEnabled is false
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Create a scheduled job with isEnabled=false
2. Set nextRun to past time
3. Wait for scheduler tick
Expected Result:
1. Disabled job is NOT picked up by scheduler
2. No ScheduleJobRun is created
3. nextRun remains unchanged

Title: Check scheduler should stop triggering after endDate has passed
Test Data: Job with endDate in the past
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Create a job with endDate set to yesterday
2. Wait for scheduler tick
Expected Result:
1. Job is not triggered after endDate
2. No new ScheduleJobRun is created
3. Job remains in list but is no longer scheduled

Title: Check scheduler should stop triggering after runUntilTimes limit is reached
Test Data: Job with runUntilTimes=3 / already run 3 times
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Create a job with runUntilTimes=3
2. Run the job 3 times
3. Wait for scheduler tick after 3rd run
Expected Result:
1. After 3rd run / job is no longer triggered
2. runTimes matches runUntilTimes (3)
3. No additional ScheduleJobRun is created

Title: Check runTimes should increment by 1 after each trigger
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Trigger a job multiple times
2. Check runTimes value after each trigger
Expected Result:
1. ScheduleJob.step.trigger.runTimes increments by 1
2. Count accurately reflects total number of runs
3. Value persists across scheduler restarts

Title: Check lastRun should be updated to trigger datetime after each trigger
Preconditions: EkoAI scheduler is running / At least one enabled job exists
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. Check ScheduleJob.step.trigger.lastRun value
Expected Result:
1. lastRun is updated to the exact trigger datetime
2. Timestamp is accurate and persisted
3. Value is visible in job configuration page

Title: Check individual request should be dispatched for each audience user to external endpoint
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job with 5 audience users configured
2. Monitor requests sent to external endpoint
Expected Result:
1. Five separate requests are sent to the process endpoint
2. Each request contains unique scheduleJobRunUserId
3. Each request includes user context data

Title: Check API key header should be included in outbound request to external endpoint
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. Inspect the outbound request to external endpoint
Expected Result:
1. Request contains X-API-KEY header
2. Header value matches the configured apiKey
3. Key is sent securely in the header

Title: Check HMAC signature should be included in outbound request header
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. Inspect the outbound request headers
Expected Result:
1. EkoAI-Signature header is present
2. Signature is HMAC-SHA256 computed over the JSON payload
3. External server can verify the signature with shared key

Title: Check user status should advance to EXECUTING when endpoint returns 200/202
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. External endpoint returns HTTP 200 OK
Expected Result:
1. ScheduleJobRunUser.status advances to EXECUTING
2. System awaits callback from external service
3. Processing timestamp is recorded

Title: Check request payload should contain guaranteed user fields
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. Inspect the outbound request payload
Expected Result:
1. Payload contains: id (scheduleJobRunUserId)
2. data.userId is present
3. data.username is present
4. Guaranteed fields are always included

Title: Check ScheduleJobRunUser record should be created per user when job triggers
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job with N audience users
2. Inspect the created records
Expected Result:
1. N ScheduleJobRunUser records are created
2. Each record has initial status PENDING
3. Records are linked to the parent ScheduleJobRun

Title: Check user status should be FAILED immediately when endpoint returns 4xx
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. External endpoint returns HTTP 400 or 403
Expected Result:
1. ScheduleJobRunUser.status is set to FAILED
2. failReasonCode = VALIDATION_ERROR
3. No automatic retry is attempted for 4xx errors

Title: Check request should retry with backoff when endpoint returns 5xx
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. External endpoint returns HTTP 500
Expected Result:
1. EkoAI retries up to configured retry times (default 3)
2. Backoff delay increases between retries
3. If all retries exhausted / status is set to FAILED

Title: Check request should be treated as failure when endpoint does not respond within 10 seconds
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. External endpoint does not respond within 10 seconds
Expected Result:
1. Timeout is treated as transient failure
2. Request is retried up to retry times
3. If retries exhausted / user status is FAILED

Title: Check slow-start throttling should increase concurrency on success for large audience
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job with 100+ audience users
2. Monitor request dispatch pattern
Expected Result:
1. System starts with low concurrency (slow-start)
2. Concurrency increases as successful responses are received
3. Concurrency decreases on failures
4. Large audience is processed without overwhelming the endpoint

Title: Check default timeout should be 100 seconds when not configured
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Create a job with default timeout
2. Trigger the job
3. External callback does not arrive within 100 seconds
Expected Result:
1. After 100 seconds / ScheduleJobRunUser is marked FAILED
2. Default timeout value is applied
3. Timeout is configurable per job

Title: Check custom timeout should be respected when set to 300 seconds
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Create a job with timeoutSeconds=300
2. Trigger the job
3. Wait for callback
Expected Result:
1. System waits up to 300 seconds for callback
2. Timeout is extended from default
3. No failure is triggered before 300 seconds

Title: Check optional user fields should be included when available in payload
Test Data: User with email / firstname / lastname / position
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job with audience user that has full profile
2. Inspect outbound request payload
Expected Result:
1. Optional fields included: email / firstname / lastname
2. Additional fields: position / ad_domain / extras
3. Missing optional fields are omitted (not null)

Title: Check stuck PROCESSING users should be marked FAILED after 24-hour backstop
Preconditions: Job has been triggered / External endpoint is configured
Steps:
1. Trigger a job
2. User remains in PROCESSING state for more than 24 hours
Expected Result:
1. Background scheduler identifies stuck record
2. ScheduleJobRunUser is automatically marked FAILED
3. Failure reason indicates timeout backstop

Title: Check user status should update to FINISHED when callback returns success with result
Preconditions: Process step has dispatched requests / External service is processing
Steps:
1. External service sends POST callback
2. Payload contains status='success' and result.homePage
Expected Result:
1. ScheduleJobRunUser.run.process.status set to FINISHED
2. result.homePage content is stored
3. User is ready for action step

Title: Check correct user record should be updated when callback echoes matching ID
Preconditions: Process step has dispatched requests / External service is processing
Steps:
1. External service sends callback
2. Payload contains id matching scheduleJobRunUserId
Expected Result:
1. System matches callback to correct ScheduleJobRunUser
2. Only the matching record is updated
3. Other records remain unchanged

Title: Check callback should be accepted when X-API-KEY header is valid
Preconditions: Process step has dispatched requests / External service is processing
Steps:
1. External service sends callback request
2. Request includes correct X-API-KEY header
Expected Result:
1. Request is accepted and processed
2. ScheduleJobRunUser record is updated
3. HTTP 200 response is returned

Title: Check result.homePage should be stored when callback includes homepage content
Preconditions: Process step has dispatched requests / External service is processing
Steps:
1. External service sends callback with result.homePage content
2. Observe ScheduleJobRunUser record
Expected Result:
1. run.process.result.homePage is populated
